full_text:
Continuous Integration and Deployment Pipelines for
Quantum Software
Abstract— Applying DevOps practices to quantum software development requires adapting continuous
integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines to accommodate quantum simulators, hardware
queues, and probabilistic test oracles. We present QFlow, an open-source CI/CD framework for quantum
software that integrates noise-aware simulation, hardware-in-the-loop testing, and statistical flakiness
detection. Evaluation across seven quantum software projects shows that QFlow reduces the mean time
to detect regressions by 63% compared to ad-hoc testing practices, while keeping pipeline execution time
under 10 minutes for projects with up to 50 quantum circuits.
1. Introduction
CI/CD is a cornerstone of modern software construction and engineering process. Quantum software
poses unique challenges for pipelines: tests are slow on real hardware, and results are stochastic.
2. QFlow Design
QFlow provides a YAML-based DSL for specifying quantum pipeline stages, including simulator selection,
shot count, and acceptance thresholds.
3. Statistical Flakiness Detection
We use the chi-squared test to distinguish genuine regressions from statistical noise in quantum
measurement outcomes.
4. Evaluation
Seven projects were migrated to QFlow. Regression detection improved by 63%.
5. Conclusion
QFlow demonstrates that CI/CD practices can be adapted to quantum software with modest changes to
existing pipeline tooling.

We present QFlow, an open-source CI/CD framework for quantum software that integrates noise-aware simulation, hardware-in-the-loop testing, and statistical flakiness detection. Evaluation across seven quantum software projects shows that QFlow reduces the mean time to detect regressions by 63% compared to ad-hoc testing practices, while keeping pipeline execution time under 10 minutes for projects with up to 50 quantum circuits. 1. Introduction CI/CD is a cornerstone of modern software construction and engineering process. Quantum software poses unique challenges for pipelines: tests are slow on real hardware, and results are stochastic. 2. QFlow Design QFlow provides a YAML-based DSL for specifying quantum pipeline stages, including simulator selection, shot count, and acceptance thresholds. 3. Statistical Flakiness Detection We use the chi-squared test to distinguish genuine regressions from statistical noise in quantum measurement outcomes. 4. Evaluation Seven projects were migrated to QFlow. Regression detection improved by 63%. 5. Conclusion QFlow demonstrates that CI/CD practices can be adapted to quantum software with modest changes to existing pipeline tooling.
